name: enforce her will
shortDescription: Enforce Her Will is an aspect or title associated with Ereshkigal, the Sumerian queen of the underworld, Kur. It emphasizes her absolute authority and the inescapable nature of her decrees within her domain.
description: The phrase "enforce her will" is not the name of a distinct deity but rather a descriptor of Ereshkigal's power and reign in the Sumerian underworld. Ereshkigal, meaning "Queen of the Great Below," ruled Kur, the land of the dead, with absolute authority. Her will was law, and no one, mortal or divine, could escape her judgment within her realm. The phrase highlights the inescapable nature of death and the power Ereshkigal wielded over the deceased. While not a separate entity, the concept of "enforce her will" is crucial to understanding Ereshkigal's role and the Sumerian perception of the afterlife. Her power extended over disease, justice, and the souls of the departed. The descent of Inanna, Ereshkigal's sister, into Kur is a primary narrative showcasing Ereshkigal's dominion and the consequences of defying her authority. Inanna's attempt to usurp her sister's throne resulted in her death and subsequent resurrection only through the intervention of Enki. This myth reinforces the unyielding nature of Ereshkigal's power and the absolute necessity to respect her will within the underworld. Her court was filled with demons and lesser deities who carried out her commands, ensuring that her will was always enforced. Ereshkigal embodies the inevitability of death and the acceptance of one's fate in the afterlife.

sources.title: Myths of the Ancient Near East
sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer

sources.title: Ancient Near Eastern Texts Relating to the Old Testament
sources.author: James B. Pritchard
